# UC-22.11.77 · IDS and IPS Alert Volume Baseline and Spike Detection

## Description

Applies moving baselines to IDS/IPS alert rates on CDE perimeter sensors so intrusion-detection failures or sudden drops in alerting—often a sign of sensor issues—are visible alongside spikes.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS, PCI-DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ids and sourcetypes pan:threat, suricata:alert. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Tune `med>10` for low-noise signatures. (2) Pair drops with health checks. (3) Feed spikes to SOC shift lead. (4) Include in monthly IDS tuning minutes. Indexes required: `index=ids`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:threat`, `sourcetype=suricata:alert`; Lookups: `pci_sensor_zones`, `pci_sensor_zones.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ids` `pan:threat`; `suricata:alert`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tune `med>10` for low-noise signatures. (2) Pair drops with health checks. (3) Feed spikes to SOC shift lead. (4) Include in monthly IDS tuning minutes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ids sourcetype IN ("pan:threat","suricata:alert") earliest=-30d
| lookup pci_sensor_zones.csv sensor OUTPUT cde_adjacent
| where cde_adjacent="true"
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count by _time, signature
| eventstats median(count) as med by signature
| eval spike=if(count>med*4 AND med>10,1,0)
| where spike=1
| table _time, signature, count, med
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**IDS and IPS Alert Volume Baseline and Spike Detection (PCI DSS Req 11.4.1, 11.4.4)** — Applies moving baselines to IDS/IPS alert rates on CDE perimeter sensors so intrusion-detection failures or sudden drops in alerting—often a sign of sensor issues—are visible alongside spikes.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ids` `pan:threat`; `suricata:alert`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ids.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ids, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where cde_adjacent="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` aggregates events by **_time, signature**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by signature** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **spike** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where spike=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.signature span=1d | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**IDS and IPS Alert Volume Baseline and Spike Detection (PCI DSS Req 11.4.1, 11.4.4)** — Applies moving baselines to IDS/IPS alert rates on CDE perimeter sensors so intrusion-detection failures or sudden drops in alerting—often a sign of sensor issues—are visible alongside spikes.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ids` `pan:threat`; `suricata:alert`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: _time, count, med, signature, spike.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line (count by signature top-N), table (spike rows), single value (spike days).

## SPL

```spl
index=ids sourcetype IN ("pan:threat","suricata:alert") earliest=-30d
| lookup pci_sensor_zones.csv sensor OUTPUT cde_adjacent
| where cde_adjacent="true"
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count by _time, signature
| eventstats median(count) as med by signature
| eval spike=if(count>med*4 AND med>10,1,0)
| where spike=1
| table _time, signature, count, med
| sort - count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.Intrusion_Detection by Intrusion_Detection.signature, Intrusion_Detection.dest, Intrusion_Detection.severity | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Line (count by signature top-N), table (spike rows), single value (spike days).
